Federal Compliance

FedRAMP Authorization
& Cloud ATO Services

Authorization to Operate (ATO) support for cloud service providers pursuing federal market access — from readiness assessment through authorization and continuous monitoring.

FedRAMP Authorization Support for Cloud Service Providers

FedRAMP (Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program) is the federal government's standardized security authorization framework for cloud products and services. If your organization provides cloud services to federal agencies — or wants to — FedRAMP authorization is either required or strongly preferred by procurement officers across DoD and civilian agencies.

Achieving authorization is a multi-year, resource-intensive process. We support cloud service providers (CSPs) at every phase — from initial readiness gap assessment through full authorization and the ongoing continuous monitoring obligations that follow.

Authorization Pathways

FedRAMP offers multiple authorization pathways. We advise on which is appropriate for your business model, existing customer base, and timeline:

  • Agency Authorization — Sponsored by a specific federal agency; typically faster and more common for CSPs with an established agency customer
  • JAB Authorization (P-ATO) — Provisional authorization from the Joint Authorization Board (DoD, DHS, GSA); highest recognition across all agencies but highly competitive
  • FedRAMP Ready — Pre-authorization designation confirming readiness for the full process; useful for business development before full ATO
  • FedRAMP In-Process — Official status designation during active authorization; opens procurement doors while authorization proceeds

Our FedRAMP Engagement Services

  • Readiness Assessment — Gap analysis against FedRAMP High/Moderate/Low baselines; identify control gaps, estimate remediation effort, and build an authorization roadmap
  • System Security Plan (SSP) Development — Authoring and review of the primary authorization artifact — the SSP — covering all 325+ controls (Moderate baseline)
  • Control Implementation — Advisory and implementation support for technical and operational control gaps identified in the readiness assessment
  • 3PAO Coordination — Selection guidance and coordination with Third Party Assessment Organizations (3PAOs) conducting your Security Assessment Report (SAR)
  • POA&M Management — Structured Plan of Action & Milestones tracking and remediation support through the authorization process
  • Agency Sponsorship Support — Guidance on identifying and engaging prospective agency sponsors; preparing the business case for sponsorship
  • Continuous Monitoring (ConMon) — Post-authorization monthly vulnerability scanning, annual assessment coordination, and deviation/incident reporting as required by FedRAMP ConMon requirements
  • Significant Change Requests (SCR) — Advisory support when proposing significant changes to an authorized system boundary

Impact Level Baseline Support

FedRAMP baselines correspond to the NIST 800-53 impact levels. We support authorization across all three:

  • Low Baseline — 125 controls; appropriate for systems where loss of CIA would have limited adverse effect
  • Moderate Baseline — 325 controls; the most common baseline, covering the vast majority of federal cloud procurements
  • High Baseline — 421 controls; required for systems processing law enforcement, emergency services, financial, or health data at high sensitivity levels

Authorization Timelines

  • Readiness: 2–4 months
  • Agency ATO: 6–18 months
  • JAB P-ATO: 12–24 months
  • ConMon: Ongoing monthly

Key Deliverables

  • System Security Plan (SSP)
  • Security Assessment Report (SAR)
  • Plan of Action & Milestones
  • ConMon monthly reports
  • Readiness assessment report

Related Frameworks

  • NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5
  • NIST SP 800-37 (RMF)
  • FIPS 199 / FIPS 200
  • FedRAMP Policy Memo (2022)

Start Your FedRAMP Journey

Most CSPs underestimate the preparation required. Start with a readiness assessment — we'll give you a clear picture of effort, timeline, and cost before you commit.
